title: New OFE Vision Paper: Open Source Software in Europe’s Telecoms Industry
content: Open source is already in Europe’s telecoms networks and software stacks. Being able to control and efficiently leverage it, as well as contribute back, is the harder question, and one the industry has faced as its use of open source has matured over the last decade. While numerous operators lead the way, the potential of open source in the telecoms industry is just beginning to be fulfilled. Sustained cooperation between the industry, public sector, and the open source ecosystem will remain critical in the years ahead as it seeks to remain sovereign and competitive.

title: From Recommendations to Actions: A Comparison of OFE’s submission of 3 February 2026 with Section 4 of COM(2026) 503 final (adopted 3 June 2026)
content:
The European Commission’s European Technological Sovereignty Package includes a full strategy for EU open digital ecosystems. OpenForum Europe submitted a contribution to the European Commission’s call for evidence on 3 February 2026 and the Commission published a comprehensive package on 3 June 2026.

The headline outcome is clear: Open Source now has a standalone EU strategy inside the technological sovereignty package, sitting next to the Chips Act 2.0 and CADA proposals. That elevation is the clearest win for OFE’s argument that Open Source should be treated as a strategic priority.  

We already provided a first reaction on the package, as well as on the Strategy for EU Open Digital Ecosystems, now it is time for a deeper look into what OFE recommended and how those recommendations are reflected in the published strategy. 

Strong alignment: The strategy matches OFE on the points where OFE pushed hardest on instruments rather than framing.

  • demand-side and procurement action including an Open Source first principle and the public sector as anchor customer; 
  • a dedicated Open Source Maintenance Instrument; 
  • an ENISA-supported list of most exposed dependencies;

creator: Laszlo Igenczi
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: Technological Sovereignty Through Open Innovation Fortifies Europe’s Security
content:
The European Commission’s European Tech Sovereignty package, published in early June, sets a far-reaching approach to steering the EU’s technological development, creating healthier markets and securing the continent’s digital autonomy.  In addition to addressing semiconductors, AI and cloud ecosystems, the package contains the first comprehensive open source strategy, which fosters and supports open innovation across industrial sectors. 

Although not directly in the focus of the European Tech Sovereignty Package, one of the sectors strongly influenced is defence and space. The defence industry is often at the forefront of technological innovation, and the European Commission’s defence strategy recognises the need to keep pace with technological change, as the geopolitical situation shifts. “Preserving Peace – Defence Readiness Roadmap 2030” was published in late 2025 and follows a white paper by the Commission and the EU’s High Representative for Foreign Affairs Kaja Kallas. The roadmap sets out a plan to enhance Europe’s security by addressing identified defence industry capability gaps in key areas by 2030.

creator: Jaakko Karhu

title: A Vision for the EU’s Open Digital Ecosystem: Beyond an Open Source Strategy
content: On 3 June 2026, the European Commission published its Open Source Strategy as part of the broader Tech Sovereignty Package. After years in which open source software was treated as a technical detail rather than a political priority, it has now arrived at the centre of Europe’s industrial and geopolitical agenda. Understanding why this shift happened and what it means matters for everyone who develops, maintains or depends on open source software in Europe.

creator: Aimilia Givropoulou
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: The Path Towards Open Strategic Autonomy: OFE Welcomes the European Commission’s Tech Sovereignty Package
content:
OpenForum Europe (OFE) welcomes the adoption of the European Commission’s Tech Sovereignty Package. As part of this package, the European Commission has now, for the first time, a comprehensive strategy on open source. With this strategy the European Commission recognises that open source crucially contributes to Europe’s  technological sovereignty, competitiveness, and innovation.

We welcome this package as a defining moment for open source in Europe, one that OpenForum Europe and the wider community have worked towards for more than two decades. OFE’s belief in openness, open source and open standards as the foundation of a sovereign digital Europe, is what drives us forward. The strategy represents Europe’s first comprehensive framework and reference point for open source. We look forward to collaborating with the community in supporting the institutions implementing the strategy with the highest ambition level.” – Laszlo Igneczi, OFE’s Executive Director

title: Global Open Source Collaboration in Action
content:
OpenForum Europe and the UN Open Source Week 2025

UN launched the UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report at OpenForum Europe’s EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026. This marks the fourth collaboration between the UN and OFE. With the 2026 edition approaching on 22–26 June 2026, revisiting the 2025 conference report provides valuable insights, inspiration, and a clear sense of what to expect.

Omar Mohsine (Open Source Coordinator, UN-ODET) and Arnau Monterde (Director of Participation and Innovation for Democracy, Barcelona City Council) joined Sachiko Muto (Chair, OpenForum Europe) to launch the report, authored by Muto. 

The report highlights the key themes and outcomes of UN Open Source Week 2025, showcasing how open source has become a cornerstone of multilateral digital cooperation. With the 2026 edition approaching on 22–26 June 2026, revisiting the 2025 conference report provides valuable insights, inspiration, and a clear sense of what to expect.

UN Open Source Week 2025: A Global Collaboration
snippet: OpenForum Europe and the UN Open Source Week 2025
creator: Christian Kummer
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: The EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026: Key learnings and takeaways
content:
The EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026, held on 30 January in Brussels under the theme ‘Digital Sovereignty Runs on Open Source’, brought together European policymakers, industry leaders, and open source advocates to address how open technologies can deliver on Europe’s competitiveness and sovereignty ambitions as part of the EU Open Source Week. This year’s Summit also announced a key transition: the appointment of Laszlo Igneczi as OpenForum Europe‘s new Executive Director.

The event was well-timed, given the announcement in early January of a new Call for Evidence on the topic of Open Digital Ecosystems, which will inform the development of the European Commission’s new open source software strategy and related actions. The consultation closed on 3 February and brought a lot of momentum to the conversations at the Summit.

creator: Nicholas Gates

title: Connecting Europe’s Open Source Community: EU Open Source Week 2026
content:
Every year, at the end of January, the global open source and open technology community descends on Brussels, on the occasion of the FOSDEM conference. Since 2025, the EU Open Source Week has grown, including OpenForum Europe’s (OFE) flagship event: The EU Open Source Policy Summit, offering a unique opportunity to connect with policymakers, community members, businesses, civil society and developers.

OFE is proud to have been involved in many activities throughout the EU Open Source Week 2026, which featured over 35 events on the official EU Open Source Week platform. To make the week more connected than ever, OFE also launched a Networking Platform, which highlighted all events, increased their visibility, and facilitated connections among many participants.

Now that the EU Open Source Week 2026 has ended, we reflect on a week full of events and activities.
